content.text:
**Category:** POC
**Target Date:** [TBD -- Weeks 1-2]
**Owner:** Dynamo + DMSi engineering (per sprint)
**Status:** [TBD] — see Jira Epic **WSA-3267** (create under **WSA-2656** if not present); all Stories with **`[POC]`** summary prefix belong under this Epic.
**Source:** Pre-S0 engineering band (visual timeline weeks 1–2); proves how the pipeline will run, connect, and be operated before foundation/security baseline work (**PA-OC-01**).

#### Success Criteria

- [ ] A working **proof-of-concept** demonstrates the intended **infrastructure and architecture** for the NGINX config pipeline (runner reachability, repo integration approach, and at least one path that exercises build/deploy or equivalent automation consistent with the target design).
- [ ] **Leadership-visible evidence** exists in Jira (Stories/Sub-tasks under Epic **WSA-3267**) that the approach, tooling, and access model are validated or explicitly risk-documented.
- [ ] **PA-OC-01** may proceed without duplicating unresolved infrastructure unknowns that fall within this POC scope.

#### Deliverables

| ID | Deliverable | Owner | Due |
|----|-------------|-------|-----|
| PA-OC-00.1 | NGINX pipeline POC — infrastructure & architecture proof | Dynamo + DMSi | [TBD] |

**Notes:** Historical Jira Story **WSA-3719** (`[POC]`) maps to this outcome; after the Epic exists, that Story (and any other **`[POC]`**-prefixed Stories) should sit **under Epic WSA-3267**. Runner/tooling spike work that was previously discussed under **PA-OC-03** remains in the WBS as **PA-OC-03.3**; only scope that truly belongs to the pre-foundation POC band stays under **PA-OC-00**.

content.text:
### Overview
The work associated with this ticket will complete a working CI/CD pipeline.  This project can be use by Dynamo to demonstrate a fully operation CI/CD process from start to finish to clients.  It will also be usable as a template for implementing a CI/CD pipelines in Dynamo client environments.   
Included will be a working example of: 
- Use of Terraform/Ansible to check the validity of the deployment
- Use of a pipeline for deployment
- hydration of configuration files
- successful deployment of an application to Dynamo's AWS account

**Goal:** Build as nginx reference pipeline end to end. One runner deploys one nginx config change to the Dynamo AWS environment.

| Task | Owner | Deliverable |
|---|---|---|
| Deploy AWS VPC with subnets | Dynamo Engineering Team | VPC live, subnets created via IaC | 
| Build Linux runner AMI | Dynamo Engineering Team | AMI with runner agent and basic tools |
| Register one self-hosted runner | Dynamo Engineering Team | Runner appears in GitHub org settings |
| Create nginx configuration repository | Dynamo Engineering Team | Repo with current nginx configs and branch protection |
| Create nginx syntax validation workflow | Dynamo Engineering Team | PR triggers nginx -t check on runner |
| Create nginx deployment workflow | Dynamo Engineering Team | Merge triggers deploy with approval gate |
| Test full cycle: PR, validate, approve, deploy | Dynamo Engineering Team | Nginx config change deployed to AWS  via a pipeline |

**Exit Criteria:** A nginx configuration change flows from pull request through validation, approval, and deployment to a Dynamo AWS server. This is the proven reference pipeline.

**Proof of Concept Success Metric:** End-to-end nginx deployment verified within 30 days.

content.text:
This ticket is a sub ticket for the [Phase 0: Discovery and Prerequisites (Week 1-2)](https://github.com/DynamoLLC-Hub/dynamo-toolbox/blob/client-specific-doc/Client%20Notes/DMSi_AWS_Self_Hosted_GitHub_Runners_Spec.md#phase-0-discovery-and-prerequisites-week-1-2) work.  This is mainly information discovery to clear the way and set up Phase 1 of the work to be completed for the DMSi CI/CD pipeline.

**Task:** Confirm Tierpoint network details (see [Section 5.2](https://github.com/DynamoLLC-Hub/dynamo-toolbox/blob/client-specific-doc/Client%20Notes/DMSi_AWS_Self_Hosted_GitHub_Runners_Spec.md#52-action-required-tierpoint-network-discovery))
**DMSi Team to Coordinate with:** Engineering Manager  
**Will Complete:** IP ranges, firewall model, VPN readiness. 

The Tierpoint IP ranges must be confirmed before finalizing the design DMSi implementation of GitHub runners.  

[5.3 AWS VPC Design](https://github.com/DynamoLLC-Hub/dynamo-toolbox/blob/client-specific-doc/Client%20Notes/DMSi_AWS_Self_Hosted_GitHub_Runners_Spec.md#53-aws-vpc-design)

| Component | CIDR Range | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| VPC | 10.100.0.0/16 | AWS CI/CD environment |
| Runner Subnet (Private) | 10.100.1.0/24 | GitHub Actions runners |
| Monitoring Subnet (Private) | 10.100.2.0/24 | Monitoring platform |
| Management Subnet (Private) | 10.100.3.0/24 | Bastion host and admin access |

**Note:** CIDR ranges must not overlap with Tierpoint IP ranges. The Tierpoint IP ranges must be confirmed before finalizing this design.

fields.summary: Confirm Tierpoint network details (GP#13)

work_items.summary: [PA-OC-00] Infrastructure & Architecture POC
work_items.description:
**Category:** POC
**Target Date:** [TBD -- Weeks 1-2]
**Owner:** Dynamo + DMSi engineering (per sprint)
**Status:** [TBD] — see Jira Epic **WSA-3267** (create under **WSA-2656** if not present); all Stories with **`[POC]`** summary prefix belong under this Epic.
**Source:** Pre-S0 engineering band (visual timeline weeks 1–2); proves how the pipeline will run, connect, and be operated before foundation/security baseline work (**PA-OC-01**).

#### Success Criteria

- [ ] A working **proof-of-concept** demonstrates the intended **infrastructure and architecture** for the NGINX config pipeline (runner reachability, repo integration approach, and at least one path that exercises build/deploy or equivalent automation consistent with the target design).
- [ ] **Leadership-visible evidence** exists in Jira (Stories/Sub-tasks under Epic **WSA-3267**) that the approach, tooling, and access model are validated or explicitly risk-documented.
- [ ] **PA-OC-01** may proceed without duplicating unresolved infrastructure unknowns that fall within this POC scope.

#### Deliverables

| ID | Deliverable | Owner | Due |
|----|-------------|-------|-----|
| PA-OC-00.1 | NGINX pipeline POC — infrastructure & architecture proof | Dynamo + DMSi | [TBD] |

**Notes:** Historical Jira Story **WSA-3719** (`[POC]`) maps to this outcome; after the Epic exists, that Story (and any other **`[POC]`**-prefixed Stories) should sit **under Epic WSA-3267**. Runner/tooling spike work that was previously discussed under **PA-OC-03** remains in the WBS as **PA-OC-03.3**; only scope that truly belongs to the pre-foundation POC band stays under **PA-OC-00**.

work_items.summary: [PA-OC-11] Pipeline Pattern Replication
work_items.description: Apply the proven pipeline framework (GitHub Actions workflows, runner strategy, drift detection, enforcement) to additional configuration types beyond NGINX. Each new config type follows the same pattern but with config-specific adaptations.

work_items.summary: [PA-OC-10] Pipeline Fallback Activated
work_items.description: This outcome activates if PA-OC-08 (Pipeline Reliability) validation fails after maximum iterations. Planned fallback path, not an unplanned failure response.

work_items.summary: [PA-OC-09] Config-as-Code Enforced
work_items.description: The config pipeline becomes the enforced default. No manual NGINX config changes allowed outside the pipeline for covered config types. Drift detection actively flags unauthorized changes. Team trained and following config-as-code workflow.

work_items.summary: [PA-OC-08] Pipeline Reliability Validated
work_items.description: All three NGINX pipelines (build, deploy, drift detection) must demonstrate 90%+ success rate for routine deployments before the team advances to enforcement. This gate protects against premature Config-as-Code enforcement on unreliable pipelines.

work_items.summary: [PA-OC-07] Drift Detection Operational
work_items.description: Background pipeline that periodically compares the GitHub source-of-truth against live server configs and alerts on any divergence. Provides metrics for operational scorecard and prevents unauthorized manual changes.

work_items.summary: [PA-OC-06] Deploy Pipeline Operational
work_items.description: Automated deployment of validated NGINX configs to target environments using Ansible, triggered by successful build pipeline completion. Includes pre-deploy validation, deployment execution, and post-deploy health checks.

work_items.summary: [PA-OC-05] Build Pipeline Operational
work_items.description: GitHub Actions workflow that generates and validates NGINX configs. Triggered by PR merge. Runs linting, schema validation, and templating to produce deployment-ready artifacts.

work_items.summary: [PA-OC-04] Configurations in Version Control
work_items.description: All NGINX operational configurations migrated from share drive to GitHub as single source of truth. Every config change is a commit with context. Config ownership mapped and enforced through CODEOWNERS.

work_items.summary: [PA-OC-03] Migration Path and Runner Infrastructure Defined
work_items.description: Document export methods, design repository structure, evaluate and POC GitHub runner infrastructure with connectivity to target servers, and confirm Ansible automation feasibility.

work_items.summary: [PA-OC-02] Configuration Inventory and Credential Mapping Complete
work_items.description: Inventory scoped to NGINX pipeline configs required to stand up and run this pipeline. Catalog all NGINX config files, map dependencies, identify environment-specific variations, discover and manage all credentials.

work_items.summary: [PA-OC-01] Foundation Access and Security Baseline Complete
work_items.description: Establish SSH/RDP read-only access, document NGINX deployment process, document AWS account structure and VPC topology, SOC 2 compliance requirements, data classification, Dynamo access governance, and network security controls.

work_items.summary: [POC] Internal Dynamo Pipeline POC (GP#28)
work_items.description:
### Overview
The work associated with this ticket will complete a working CI/CD pipeline.  This project can be use by Dynamo to demonstrate a fully operation CI/CD process from start to finish to clients.  It will also be usable as a template for implementing a CI/CD pipelines in Dynamo client environments.   
Included will be a working example of: 
- Use of Terraform/Ansible to check the validity of the deployment
- Use of a pipeline for deployment
- hydration of configuration files
- successful deployment of an application to Dynamo's AWS account

**Goal:** Build as nginx reference pipeline end to end. One runner deploys one nginx config change to the Dynamo AWS environment.

| Task | Owner | Deliverable |
|---|---|---|
| Deploy AWS VPC with subnets | Dynamo Engineering Team | VPC live, subnets created via IaC | 
| Build Linux runner AMI | Dynamo Engineering Team | AMI with runner agent and basic tools |
| Register one self-hosted runner | Dynamo Engineering Team | Runner appears in GitHub org settings |
| Create nginx configuration repository | Dynamo Engineering Team | Repo with current nginx configs and branch protection |
| Create nginx syntax validation workflow | Dynamo Engineering Team | PR triggers nginx -t check on runner |
| Create nginx deployment workflow | Dynamo Engineering Team | Merge triggers deploy with approval gate |
| Test full cycle: PR, validate, approve, deploy | Dynamo Engineering Team | Nginx config change deployed to AWS  via a pipeline |

**Exit Criteria:** A nginx configuration change flows from pull request through validation, approval, and deployment to a Dynamo AWS server. This is the proven reference pipeline.

**Proof of Concept Success Metric:** End-to-end nginx deployment verified within 30 days.
